title: Algonquin Power & Utilities vs. CenterPoint Energy: Which Utilities Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
author: Sara Appino
description: The article compares two utility stocks for 2026 investment. Algonquin Power & Utilities operates diverse water, gas, and electric services across North America with a 4.9% revenue growth but faces turnaround challenges and negative free cash flow. CenterPoint Energy serves 7 million customers with stronger 8.3% revenue growth and benefits from Houston's booming data center and industrial expansion. The analyst recommends CenterPoint Energy due to its genuine growth trajectory versus Algonquin's recovery mode.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company shows modest 4.9% revenue growth and 8.5% net margin, but faces significant headwinds including negative free cash flow (-$249M), high debt-to-equity ratio (1.4x), and turnaround status. While dividend yield is attractive at 4.4%, the company is in recovery mode with dividend history concerns.

Company shows modest 4.9% revenue growth and 8.5% net margin, but faces significant headwinds including negative free cash flow (-$249M), high debt-to-equity ratio (1.4x), and turnaround status. While dividend yield is attractive at 4.4%, the company is in recovery mode with dividend history concerns.

Strong 8.3% revenue growth, higher 11.2% net margin, and positioned in fast-growing Houston market benefiting from data center and industrial expansion. Analyst explicitly recommends this stock for its genuine growth trajectory. However, elevated debt-to-equity ratio (2.1x) and customer concentration risk (60% from two customers) present some concerns.

Mentioned as a major customer of CenterPoint Energy, accounting for nearly 30% of billed receivables in Houston electric segment. Represents concentration risk for CenterPoint but no direct investment thesis provided.

title: What Hurt NRG's Quarterly Earnings Despite Higher Revenue, Asset Contributions
author: Akanksha Bakshi
description: NRG Energy reported Q1 2026 earnings that missed adjusted EPS estimates at $1.48 vs. $1.77 expected, despite 19% revenue growth to $10.256 billion. Unrealized hedge losses, mild Texas weather, and higher supply costs pressured results. The company reaffirmed full-year guidance and plans $1.4 billion in shareholder returns, while expanding PJM opportunities to 2 GW and pursuing data-center projects.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Adjusted EPS of $1.48 missed analyst estimates of $1.77, adjusted net income and EBITDA declined year-over-year, operating and free cash flow turned negative, and total liquidity decreased significantly. While revenue grew and guidance was reaffirmed, the earnings miss and operational headwinds outweigh positive factors.

title: Sunrun is the Number One Provider of Energy Independence for Americans, Expanding the Nation’s Largest Residential Distributed Power Plant
author: Not Specified
description: Sunrun installed 1.5 GWh of residential battery storage in 2025, representing approximately 48% of the 3.1 GWh total U.S. residential energy storage market. The company now operates over 237,000 storage and solar systems with 4.0 GWh of networked storage capacity, equivalent to 44% of all residential energy storage installed in the U.S. since inception. Sunrun's battery fleet dispatched 18 GWh to support grids in 2025 and achieved a record 71% storage attachment rate in Q4 2025.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Sunrun demonstrated dominant market leadership with nearly 50% market share of residential energy storage installations in 2025, record storage attachment rates of 71%, significant grid support contributions (18 GWh dispatched), and strong year-over-year growth of 50%+ in Texas. The company is expanding its distributed power plant operations and forming strategic partnerships, positioning it well for continued growth.

Tesla is mentioned only as a partnership partner with Sunrun in Texas for storage-plus-solar systems. The mention is brief and contextual without specific performance metrics or strategic implications disclosed.
NRG is mentioned only as a partnership partner with Sunrun in Texas. The mention is brief and contextual without specific performance metrics or strategic implications disclosed.
Vistra is mentioned only as a partnership partner with Sunrun in Texas. The mention is brief and contextual without specific performance metrics or strategic implications disclosed.

title: NRG Energy Boosts FY26 Guidance After LS Power Deal
author: Lekha Gupta
description: NRG Energy raised its FY26 financial guidance following the completion of its LS Power asset acquisition on January 30, 2026. The company increased adjusted EBITDA guidance to $5.325-$5.825 billion and Free Cash Flow guidance to $2.800-$3.300 billion, reflecting approximately 11 months of asset ownership. The acquisition adds 18 natural gas-fired power plants with 13 GW capacity and CPower's virtual power plant platform. Despite the positive guidance raise, NRG shares traded down 0.86% on the news.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company significantly raised FY26 guidance across multiple metrics (EBITDA raised by ~$1.4B, Free Cash Flow raised by ~$0.8B) following successful completion of major LS Power acquisition that adds substantial generation capacity and strategic assets. However, stock price declined on announcement, suggesting market may have expected even stronger guidance or has concerns about integration/execution.

title: Roblox, First Solar, And League Enterprise Are Among Top 10 Large Cap Losers Last Week (Jan. 5-Jan. 9): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Ten large-cap stocks experienced significant declines during the week of January 5-9, 2026. Notable losers include Madrigal Pharmaceuticals (down 11.57%), First Solar (down 14.46% following a Jefferies downgrade), Astera Labs (down 12.18%), and Roblox (down 9.99%). Other major decliners include CoStar Group, American International Group, NRG Energy, Las Vegas Sands, Johnson Controls International, and HDFC Bank. The declines were driven by analyst downgrades, leadership transitions, and executive changes.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock lost 11.57% during the week with no specific catalyst mentioned, indicating broader market weakness or company-specific concerns.
Stock declined 14.46% following a Jefferies downgrade from Buy to Hold with price target reduction from $269 to $260.
Stock fell 10.68% as multiple analysts lowered their price forecasts on the company.
Stock decreased 10.05% following announcement of CEO Peter Zaffino's planned retirement by mid-2026 and Barclays analyst downgrade of price target from $88 to $81.
Stock slumped 8.93% following executive leadership changes including CEO appointment and president stepping down.
Stock fell 9.57% during the week with no specific catalyst mentioned in the article.
Stock decreased 9.99% during the week, with partner Super League Enterprise acquiring marketing technology firm and Wells Fargo analyst lowering price target from $141 to $107.
Stock lost 12.18% during the week with no specific catalyst mentioned in the article.
Stock slumped 9.88% during the week despite Barclays analyst maintaining Equal-Weight rating and raising price target from $119 to $125.
Stock fell 5.44% during the week with no specific catalyst mentioned in the article.
While the stock is listed among losers, the article highlights a positive development (acquisition of Let's Bounce), suggesting mixed sentiment despite the weekly decline.
